response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer with 15+ years of experience, but does not meet the core hard requirements for this embedded security role. There is no evidence of native Japanese proficiency (a required qualification), no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or source modification work, and only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments roughly a decade ago. The bulk of experience is in JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and web platforms — not embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X.
strengths: Long, varied software engineering career with staff/senior-level roles
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on a developer-tools product
Strong testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD, workshops on testing and refactoring)
concerns: No native Japanese proficiency — explicit hard requirement is unmet
No demonstrated Linux kernel analysis or source-code modification experience
No experience with embedded networking, switch firmware, MACsec, 802.1X, or related security stacks

key_factors.factor: No embedded/LAN-switch security or networking background
Limited and dated C-on-UNIX experience vs. required strong proficiency
